design_philosophy.core_loop: Turn-based batting/pitching with real-time fielding; cycle through offensive and defensive innings across multiple game modes (exhibition, season, All-Star)
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Timing-based hitting mechanics against varied pitcher patterns; defensive positioning and reaction to ball placement
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Experience authentic MLB baseball with realistic stadiums, team rosters, and the satisfaction of hitting home runs in a responsive arcade-style baseball simulation
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Prioritizes arcade-style gameplay and visual authenticity over simulation depth; accepts generic stadiums to preserve arcade feel; embraces colorful alternate uniforms as feature rather than pursuing strict realism

difficulty.continues_system: Turn-based structure allows unlimited retries within innings; no traditional continue system
difficulty.checkpointing: Multi-game season mode with persistent progress across 162-game campaign; home run derby and exhibition play provide isolated challenges
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Game difficulty likely adjustable via AI opponent selection and game mode choice (exhibition vs season), though specific difficulty settings not documented

progression.unlock_mechanism: Playing through full 162-game season unlocks ability to face 1998 expansion teams (Tampa Bay Devil Rays and Arizona Diamondbacks) in subsequent playthroughs
replayability_hooks: 28 MLB teams with distinct rosters to play as/against
Home run derby mode within All-Star Game mode
Franchise mode for persistent season management
Alternate uniform system with random computer selection of home/away or colored uniforms
Multiple game modes: exhibition, season, All-Star, MLB Challenge

world_building_techniques: Game title derived from 1995 ALDS winning play by Ken Griffey Jr. (narrative framing device)
Licensed inclusion of 28 authentic MLB teams and stadiums (environmental/contextual authenticity)
Ken Griffey Jr. as sole player with actual name; other players generic (narrative constraint reflecting licensing limitations)

faction_indicators: 28 MLB teams represent distinct factions with implied regional/historical identities
environmental_storytelling: Authentic stadium designs (Wrigley Field ivy visible, Astrodome natural grass) communicate team identity and baseball history
Home/away uniform distinctions and alternate colored uniforms reinforce team branding

sources_used: wikipedia - Ken Griffey Jr.'s Winning Run (gameplay description, team rosters, unlock mechanics, game modes, reception context)
